headline: Wall Street Is Missing the Bigger Picture: Why This Legacy Tech Stalwart Is a Screaming Buy Right Now
summary: International Business Machines (NYSE:IBM) is a stock worth owning for decades because it pairs a 110-year operating history with a recurring-revenue software and infrastructure engine that is now compounding cash at an accelerating pace. IBM is built for retirement portfolios that need durability, income, and survivability across every market cycle, not narrative-driven upside. Pillar One: ... headline: IBM CEO Krishna on Quantum Advantage, Profit Strategy
summary: IBM CEO Arvind Krishna says he's excited about the potential uses for quantum computing. headline: How Much Upside Can ORCL Stock Deliver?
summary: At $211.82, Oracle (ORCL) looks set up for roughly 34% of upside over the next three years under a conservative scenario. Not a moonshot, but enough to matter if the math holds. Revenue compounding does all the work; the multiple is asked to do nothing. Here is the operational reality the math is built on.

headline: Will Oracle's AI Gamble Pay Off This Quarter?
summary: Oracle (NYSE: ORCL) is set to report its fiscal Q4 2026 earnings on Wednesday, June 10, after the market close. Heading into the results, the stock has experienced significant volatility, dropping 12.9% over the past week as investors weigh immense cloud growth against massive capital expenditures.

headline: Trump praised IBM's 'legend' CEO, government floated a $1B quantum award — is the stock still a 'very nice price'?
summary: At a December White House roundtable, Trump called IBM's chief 'a legend' and praised the stock's 'very nice price.' Months later his administration floated a proposed $1B quantum award and shares popped 13% in a week to a record. Here's where analysts see it going — and the warning sign buried in the deal.
